content: PyGithub 2.9.1 is a sync-only library — it wraps requests under the hood, has no async support, and would require run_in_executor to use inside FastAPI async route handlers. This makes it a poor fit for an async-first FastAPI app with SQLAlchemy async. gidgethub 5.4.0 is an async-native GitHub API library with a pluggable HTTP backend — it ships an httpx backend (gidgethub.httpx) allowing it to reuse the project's existing httpx dependency. It was created by Brett Cannon (CPython core dev, GitHub employee) and is used in production by the CPython bot and other GitHub automation. However, gidgethub's API is lower-level than PyGithub — it maps 1:1 to GitHub REST endpoints with dict responses rather than typed ORM-style objects. For the PECP use case (create team, create repo, add member — a small, well-defined set of operations), raw httpx calls via an authenticated async client are simplest and involve no new dependencies. The right choice is raw httpx with an httpx.AsyncClient configured with Bearer token auth headers, reusing the existing httpx >=0.28 dependency already in pyproject.toml.
